key_insight: WEST is an open-source, highly scalable excited-state simulation code that unifies GW, BSE, TDDFT, and defect embedding without explicit virtual states.
engineering_relevance: It enables predictive excited-state calculations on thousand-atom systems with GPU-accelerated strong scaling, making it useful for materials discovery and large-scale screening.
method: Combines density functional and density matrix perturbation theory to avoid explicit virtual electronic states.
Uses low-rank dielectric screening/exact-exchange representations plus localization to improve scaling.
Implements hierarchical parallelization and GPU acceleration for near-ideal scaling on thousands of GPUs.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports representative applications across spin defects, self-trapped excitons, and liquid water/ice, including systems with more than a thousand atoms and near-ideal strong scaling to thousands of GPUs.
watch_item: Performance and usability may depend on careful workflow integration and hardware access, especially for very large GPU-enabled runs and cross-package interoperability.
paper_title: The WEST code for large-scale excited-state materials simulations
